title: JavaScript ES6 Classes
response:
ES6 classes provide a cleaner syntax for creating objects and implementing inheritance. They are syntactic sugar over prototype-based inheritance — under the hood, the same prototype chain is used.

Key features:
• constructor() — runs when new is called.
• Methods defined in the class body are placed on the prototype.
• extends — sets up prototype chain inheritance.
• super() — must be called in a child constructor before using this.
• Static methods — called on the class itself, not instances.
• Private fields (#) — truly private, only accessible inside the class (ES2022).
